skill.name: decision-mapping
skill.description: Turn a loose idea into a sequenced map of investigation tickets, then drive them to resolution one at a time.
skill.summary: Turn a loose idea into a sequenced map of investigation tickets, then drive them to resolution one a...
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as command execution and text fragments as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, shell invocation, network access, credential access, or prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md. The skill is publishable with low residual risk because it asks agents to read and update a planning document.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown fences and inline code formatting as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines define an example decision-map format and a field name, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown documentation or inline code formatting. No command string, interpreter call, or execution API is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Token Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography based on text matches in ordinary words about decisions and design. No cryptographic function, hashing API, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text appears in normal prose and skill metadata. There is no code path that performs encryption, hashing, signing, or authentication.

content.value_statement: Complex ideas often stall because the open decisions are unclear. This skill turns uncertainty into a compact decision map and resolves tickets one session at a time.
actual_capabilities: Creates a compact markdown decision map for an unclear idea.
Breaks planning work into numbered investigation tickets.
Labels tickets as research, prototype, or discussion tasks.
Tracks blocking relationships between decision tickets.
Updates resolved tickets and adds newly discovered follow-up work.
Supports resuming a specific ticket from an existing decision map.
limitations: It provides planning guidance, not automatic project implementation.
It depends on accurate user context and complete project constraints.
Large maps must stay compact because each session loads the whole map.
Parallel ticket work can create merge conflicts that need human review.

use_cases.title: Plan an ambiguous product feature
use_cases.description: Turn a broad feature idea into ordered questions, dependencies, and next investigations.

use_cases.title: Structure technical discovery
use_cases.description: Separate architecture questions into research, prototype, and discussion tickets before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Resume long-running planning work
use_cases.description: Load an existing map, resolve one ticket, and record new decisions without losing prior context.

prompt_templates.title: Start a decision map
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner bootstrap for a vague idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use decision-mapping for this idea: [describe the project or feature]. Ask me one question at a time, then create the first decision map.

prompt_templates.title: Map technical unknowns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use decision-mapping to identify the main technical decisions for [project]. Separate research, prototype, and discussion tickets.

prompt_templates.title: Resume a ticket
prompt_templates.prompt: Use decision-mapping with the map at [path]. Resolve ticket #[number], update its answer, and add any newly discovered tickets.

prompt_templates.title: Refine dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup for a mature map.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this decision map for unclear dependencies, oversized tickets, and resolved decisions that invalidate other nodes. Propose precise map edits.

output_examples.input: I need to decide how our internal analytics tool should store events.
output_examples.output: A decision map with tickets for storage model choice, retention policy, query needs, ingestion volume, and prototype validation.

output_examples.input: Resume ticket #3 from our decision map about user permissions.
output_examples.output: A resolved ticket answer, updated blocking relationships, and new follow-up tickets for unanswered permission edge cases.

output_examples.input: This planning map feels too large and hard to resume.
output_examples.output: A shorter map structure with smaller ticket scopes, clearer blockers, and resolved items moved into concise answers.

best_practices: Keep each ticket small enough for one focused agent session.
Link supporting assets instead of copying long research notes into the map.
Resolve only one ticket per resume session unless the map says otherwise.
anti_patterns: Do not use the map as a full product requirements document.
Do not create large tickets that mix research, prototype work, and implementation.
Do not keep invalidated decisions after new evidence changes the plan.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a compact markdown decision map with numbered tickets and dependencies.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a project has several open decisions that cannot be solved in one session.

faq.question: Does it implement the final plan?
faq.answer: No. It organizes investigation work and records decisions before implementation.

faq.question: Can it work with existing maps?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the map path and a ticket number to resume that specific decision.

faq.question: How does it handle parallel work?
faq.answer: It expects other agents may edit the map and keeps the map as the shared source of truth.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
